subject: UX & Research
title: Presenting Findings to Skeptics
description: A short lesson to help you apply Presenting Findings to Skeptics.

hook.text: 'You only tested 5 people. Not statistically significant.' Every UX researcher has heard this dismissal.

buildup.text: Skeptics equate research with statistics. Qualitative testing uses different logic and different standards.

discovery.text: Respond: 'If 4 of 5 can't find checkout, we don't need 1,000 to confirm the button is hard to find.'

twist.text: The best defense: invite stakeholders to watch sessions live. One struggling user eliminates most objections.

climax.text: Frame findings as risk: 'If 10% of users hit this, that's Y thousand lost transactions per month.'

punchline.text: Show risk in business terms. Numbers stakeholders care about win.

quiz.question: What is the most effective way to convince skeptical stakeholders of usability findings?
options: Invite them to observe live test sessions and frame findings as business risk
Run the same test with 10,000 users for statistical significance
Present findings only in written reports with charts
